__comment__: Action primitives for tau2/telecom, consumed by the 3 families in task_spec.json (mobile_data, no_service, mms). ESSA subtask_specs adapted for tool-calling: each base_action is a tool CALL {tool,args,when} instead of a text command. The small model runs two roles per subtask: EXECUTOR (pick the next tool call per base_actions + executor_sys_rules, restricted by Python to this subtask's tools) and STATE_UPDATER (parse the tool result into patch_ops over task_state per parse_rules + patch_ops_policy). Tool names and return-string formats were verified against src/tau2/domains/telecom/{tools.py,user_tools.py}. Several subtask_types are SHARED across families (IDENTIFY_CUSTOMER, CHECK_NETWORK_MODE, ESCALATE) — selection is by the sequence entry's subtask_type; per-task bookkeeping is by the entry's name.

__conventions__.device_tools_no_args: run_speed_test, check_network_status, check_status_bar, check_data_restriction_status, check_network_mode_preference, check_vpn_status, check_wifi_calling_status, check_apn_settings, can_send_mms, toggle_roaming, toggle_data, toggle_data_saver_mode, toggle_airplane_mode, toggle_wifi_calling, reseat_sim_card, reset_apn_settings, reboot_device, disconnect_vpn, make_payment take NO args (they operate on the user's own device; solo mode routes them to the user toolkit).
__conventions__.backend_tools: get_customer_by_phone, get_details_by_id, get_data_usage, get_bills_for_customer, enable_roaming, refuel_data, send_payment_request, resume_line, transfer_to_human_agents take backend ids (customer_id/line_id/bill_id).
__conventions__.speed_parse: run_speed_test returns 'Speed Test Result: <n> Mbps (<Desc>). ...' on success, or 'Speed test failed: No Connection.' when data is down. Map: 'No Connection'->'no connection', '(Excellent)'->'excellent', '(Good)'->'good', '(Fair)'->'fair', '(Poor)'->'poor', '(Very Poor)'->'very poor'. ALSO set data_available: false when 'no connection', true for any real speed. When speed_test=='excellent', ALSO set resolved=true.
__conventions__.toggle_caution: toggle_* tools FLIP state. Only call them when a read shows the wrong state, and at most once per fix (the base_action `when` and the retest_guard enforce this).
__conventions__.service_parse: check_network_status returns multiple lines. 'Cellular Connection: <connected|no_service|searching>' -> service_status; 'Airplane Mode: ON/OFF' -> airplane_mode; 'SIM Card Status: <active|missing|locked_pin|locked_puk>' -> sim_status. When service_status=='connected' ALSO set resolved=true.

IDENTIFY_CUSTOMER.goal_template: resolve the backend customer_id and list the customer's line_ids
executor_sys_rules: Call get_customer_by_phone ONCE with task_state.phone_number; it returns a Customer with customer_id and a line_ids list.
This subtask only identifies the customer — do NOT probe individual lines here (RESOLVE_LINE does that) and do NOT call any device tool.
parse_rules: set customer_id = Customer.customer_id.
set candidate_line_ids = the Customer's line_ids list verbatim (e.g. ["L1001","L1002","L1003"]); RESOLVE_LINE will walk it.

RESOLVE_LINE.goal_template: find the line_id whose phone_number == <phone_number> by walking candidate_line_ids one at a time
executor_sys_rules: Call get_details_by_id with id = the FIRST id in task_state.candidate_line_ids to inspect that one line.
Do NOT call get_customer_by_phone again and do NOT call any device tool here.
parse_rules: Look at the line returned by get_details_by_id.
If its phone_number == task_state.phone_number, set task_state.line_id to that line's line_id (you are done).

Otherwise it is the wrong line: set task_state.candidate_line_ids to the SAME list with its FIRST element removed (drop the id you just checked), so the next call tries a different line. Do NOT set line_id in that case.
RESOLVE_LINE.sys_output_format: { "patch_ops": [ {"op":"set","path":"task_state.line_id","value":"L1002"} ] }   (or, when the checked line did NOT match: { "patch_ops": [ {"op":"set","path":"task_state.candidate_line_ids","value":["L1002","L1003"]} ] })

executor_sys_rules: Call run_speed_test with no arguments.
This is read-only; never apply a fix here.
parse_rules: Map the result string to one speed word per __conventions__.speed_parse and set task_state.speed_test.
Set task_state.data_available = false if the result is 'no connection', otherwise true.
If the word is 'excellent',.

CHECK_NETWORK_STATUS.goal_template: read device roaming and mobile-data toggles
executor_sys_rules: Call check_network_status with no arguments.
Read-only; never apply a fix here.
parse_rules: From 'Data Roaming Enabled: Yes/No' set task_state.roaming_enabled_device (Yes->true, No->false).
From 'Mobile Data Enabled: Yes/No' set task_state.mobile_data_enabled (Yes->true, No->false).

FIX_ROAMING_DEVICE.goal_template: turn Data Roaming ON on the device, then retest

base_actions.when: toggle_roaming just returned 'Data Roaming is now ON'

executor_sys_rules: Step 1: call toggle_roaming ONLY if task_state.roaming_enabled_device is false (it flips state; do not call twice).
Step 2: after the toggle confirms 'Data Roaming is now ON.', call run_speed_test to retest.
Do not touch backend line roaming here (that is FIX_LINE_ROAMING).
parse_rules: On 'Data Roaming is now ON.' set task_state.roaming_enabled_device=true.

Set task_state.speed_test / data_available / resolved ONLY from a run_speed_test result. That result is EITHER 'Speed Test Result: <n> Mbps (<Desc>).' (success: map <Desc> -> speed word, data_available=true) OR 'Speed test failed: No Connection.' (data still down: speed_test='no connection', data_available=false). NEVER set these from a toggle/enable result — its Status Bar 'Excellent' is SIGNAL strength, NOT data speed, and means nothing here.

FIX_LINE_ROAMING.goal_template: ensure the backend LINE is roaming-enabled (at no cost), then retest
executor_sys_rules: First read the line via get_details_by_id(task_state.line_id) to learn whether roaming is already enabled on the line.
Call enable_roaming ONLY if the line is not already roaming-enabled. Enabling is free to the user.
After enable_roaming succeeds, call run_speed_test to retest.
parse_rules: From the line details set task_state.line_roaming_enabled (the line's roaming_enabled field: true/false).
After enable_roaming succeeds ('Roaming enabled successfully' or 'already enabled') set task_state.line_roaming_enabled=true.

Set task_state.speed_test / data_available / resolved ONLY from a run_speed_test result. That result is EITHER 'Speed Test Result: <n> Mbps (<Desc>).' (success: map <Desc> -> speed word, data_available=true) OR 'Speed test failed: No Connection.' (data still down: speed_test='no connection', data_available=false). NEVER set these from a toggle/enable result — its Status Bar 'Excellent' is SIGNAL strength, NOT data speed, and means nothing here.

FIX_MOBILE_DATA.goal_template: turn Mobile Data ON on the device, then retest

base_actions.when: toggle_data just turned mobile data ON

executor_sys_rules: Call toggle_data ONLY if task_state.mobile_data_enabled is false (it flips state; call at most once).
After mobile data is ON, call run_speed_test to retest.
parse_rules: When the toggle confirms 'Mobile Data is now ON.' set task_state.mobile_data_enabled=true.

Set task_state.speed_test / data_available / resolved ONLY from a run_speed_test result. That result is EITHER 'Speed Test Result: <n> Mbps (<Desc>).' (success: map <Desc> -> speed word, data_available=true) OR 'Speed test failed: No Connection.' (data still down: speed_test='no connection', data_available=false). NEVER set these from a toggle/enable result — its Status Bar 'Excellent' is SIGNAL strength, NOT data speed, and means nothing here.

CHECK_DATA_USAGE.goal_template: check whether the line's data allowance is exhausted
executor_sys_rules: Call get_data_usage once with customer_id and line_id.
Read-only; never apply a fix here.
parse_rules: Set task_state.data_usage_exceeded = (data_used_gb >= data_limit_gb + data_refueling_gb).

FIX_DATA_USAGE.goal_template: refuel 2.0 GB (the policy maximum), then retest
executor_sys_rules: Data refueling is a STANDARD remediation (telecom policy: max 2GB, no special permission needed — unlike payments).
Refuel EXACTLY 2.0 GB: call refuel_data(customer_id=task_state.customer_id, line_id=task_state.line_id, gb_amount=2.0).
Do NOT change the plan. After refuel_data succeeds, call run_speed_test to retest.
parse_rules: After refuel_data succeeds ('Successfully added ... GB') set task_state.data_usage_exceeded=false.

Set task_state.speed_test / data_available / resolved ONLY from a run_speed_test result. That result is EITHER 'Speed Test Result: <n> Mbps (<Desc>).' (success: map <Desc> -> speed word, data_available=true) OR 'Speed test failed: No Connection.' (data still down: speed_test='no connection', data_available=false). NEVER set these from a toggle/enable result — its Status Bar 'Excellent' is SIGNAL strength, NOT data speed, and means nothing here.

CHECK_DATA_SAVER.goal_template: check whether Data Saver mode is limiting speed
executor_sys_rules: Call check_data_restriction_status with no arguments.
Read-only; never apply a fix here.
parse_rules: From 'Data Saver mode is ON/OFF' set task_state.data_saver_on (ON->true, OFF->false).

FIX_DATA_SAVER.goal_template: turn Data Saver mode OFF, then retest
executor_sys_rules: Call toggle_data_saver_mode ONLY if task_state.data_saver_on is true (it flips state; call at most once).
After Data Saver is OFF, call run_speed_test to retest.
parse_rules: When the toggle confirms 'Data Saver Mode is now OFF.' set task_state.data_saver_on=false.

Set task_state.speed_test / data_available / resolved ONLY from a run_speed_test result. That result is EITHER 'Speed Test Result: <n> Mbps (<Desc>).' (success: map <Desc> -> speed word, data_available=true, resolved=true ONLY if <Desc> is Excellent) OR 'Speed test failed: No Connection.' (data still down: speed_test='no connection', data_available=false, resolved stays false). NEVER set these from a toggle/enable result — its Status Bar 'Excellent' is SIGNAL strength, NOT data speed, and means nothing here.

CHECK_NETWORK_MODE.goal_template: read the phone's preferred network mode
executor_sys_rules: Call check_network_mode_preference with no arguments.
Read-only; never apply a fix here.
parse_rules: From 'Network Mode Preference: <value>' set task_state.network_mode to that value verbatim (e.g. '2g_only', '4g_5g_preferred').

FIX_NETWORK_MODE.goal_template: set the network mode to one that includes 5G, then retest speed

base_actions.when: the network mode was just set to 4g_5g_preferred

executor_sys_rules: Call set_network_mode_preference with mode='4g_5g_preferred' (an option that includes 5G) if the current mode is an older/limiting type.
After the mode is set, call run_speed_test to retest.
parse_rules: After 'Preferred Network Mode set to: 4g_5g_preferred' set task_state.network_mode='4g_5g_preferred'.

Set task_state.speed_test / data_available / resolved ONLY from a run_speed_test result. That result is EITHER 'Speed Test Result: <n> Mbps (<Desc>).' (success: map <Desc> -> speed word, data_available=true, resolved=true ONLY if <Desc> is Excellent) OR 'Speed test failed: No Connection.' (data still down: speed_test='no connection', data_available=false, resolved stays false). NEVER set these from a toggle/enable result — its Status Bar 'Excellent' is SIGNAL strength, NOT data speed, and means nothing here.

CHECK_VPN.goal_template: check whether a VPN is active (it can degrade speed)
executor_sys_rules: Call check_vpn_status with no arguments.
Read-only; never apply a fix here.
parse_rules: If the result says 'VPN is ON and connected' set task_state.vpn_active=true; if 'VPN is turned OFF' or 'not connected' set task_state.vpn_active=false.

FIX_VPN.goal_template: disconnect the active VPN, then retest speed
executor_sys_rules: Call disconnect_vpn ONLY if task_state.vpn_active is true.
After the VPN is disconnected, call run_speed_test to retest.
parse_rules: After 'VPN disconnected successfully' set task_state.vpn_active=false.

Set task_state.speed_test / data_available / resolved ONLY from a run_speed_test result. That result is EITHER 'Speed Test Result: <n> Mbps (<Desc>).' (success: map <Desc> -> speed word, data_available=true, resolved=true ONLY if <Desc> is Excellent) OR 'Speed test failed: No Connection.' (data still down: speed_test='no connection', data_available=false, resolved stays false). NEVER set these from a toggle/enable result — its Status Bar 'Excellent' is SIGNAL strength, NOT data speed, and means nothing here.

CHECK_SERVICE.goal_template: read airplane mode, SIM status, and cellular connection status
executor_sys_rules: Call check_network_status with no arguments.
Read-only; never apply a fix here.
parse_rules: From 'Airplane Mode: ON/OFF' set task_state.airplane_mode (ON->true, OFF->false).
From 'SIM Card Status: <v>' set task_state.sim_status (one of 'active','missing','locked_pin','locked_puk').
From 'Cellular Connection: <v>' set task_state.service_status (one of 'connected','no_service','searching').
If service_status=='connected'.

FIX_AIRPLANE.goal_template: turn Airplane Mode OFF, then recheck service

base_actions.when: airplane mode was just turned OFF

executor_sys_rules: Call toggle_airplane_mode ONLY if task_state.airplane_mode is true (it flips state; call at most once).
After the toggle confirms 'Airplane Mode is now OFF.', call check_network_status to confirm service is restored.
parse_rules: On 'Airplane Mode is now OFF.' set task_state.airplane_mode=false.
On a check_network_status result set task_state.service_status from 'Cellular Connection: <v>'.

FIX_SIM.goal_template: reseat the SIM card, then recheck service
executor_sys_rules: Call reseat_sim_card ONLY if task_state.sim_status is 'missing'.
After 'SIM card re-seated successfully.', call check_network_status to confirm service is restored.
parse_rules: On 'SIM card re-seated successfully.' set task_state.sim_status='active'.
On a check_network_status result set task_state.service_status from 'Cellular Connection: <v>'.

CHECK_APN.goal_template: check whether the APN settings are broken
executor_sys_rules: Call check_apn_settings with no arguments.
Read-only; never apply a fix here.
parse_rules: From 'Current APN Name: <v>' set task_state.apn_broken = true if the name is 'broken' or 'Not Set', otherwise false (a working APN shows a normal carrier name like 'internet').

FIX_APN.goal_template: reset APN settings and reboot, then recheck service

base_actions.when: task_state.apn_broken == true and APN not yet reset
APN reset has been requested but device not yet rebooted

executor_sys_rules: Step 1: call reset_apn_settings (the reset applies on the next reboot).
Step 2: call reboot_device so the reset takes effect.
Step 3: call check_network_status to confirm service is restored.
Call each of these at most once, in this order.
parse_rules: After reset_apn_settings ('APN settings will reset at reboot.') set task_state.apn_broken=false.
Set task_state.service_status ONLY from a check_network_status 'Cellular Connection: <v>' line (NOT from the reset/reboot status-bar text).

CHECK_SUSPENSION.goal_template: check whether the line is suspended and whether its contract has ended
executor_sys_rules: Call get_details_by_id(task_state.line_id) once to read the line.
Read-only; never apply a fix here.
parse_rules: From the line's status field set task_state.line_suspended (status 'Suspended' -> true, 'Active' -> false).

You MUST ALSO set task_state.contract_ended from the line's contract_end_date in the SAME response: it is true ONLY if that date is strictly before the current date 2025-02-25, otherwise false. Examples: '2026-12-31' -> false (2026 is after 2025); '2025-06-30' -> false; '2025-01-31' or any 2024 date -> true. Never omit contract_ended.

executor_sys_rules: Call get_bills_for_customer(task_state.customer_id) once.
Read-only; never apply a fix here.
parse_rules: Set task_state.overdue_bill_id = the bill_id of the bill whose status is 'Overdue'.

REQUEST_PAYMENT.goal_template: send the customer a payment request for the overdue bill
executor_sys_rules: Call send_payment_request(customer_id, bill_id=task_state.overdue_bill_id) once. The bill is already confirmed overdue.
parse_rules: After 'Payment request sent to the customer' set task_state.payment_requested=true.

MAKE_PAYMENT.goal_template: accept the payment request to pay the overdue bill
executor_sys_rules: Call make_payment with no arguments to pay the pending payment request (the user authorized this).
parse_rules: After 'Payment of ... has been made for bill ...' set task_state.bill_paid=true.

RESUME_LINE.goal_template: resume the suspended line now that the bill is paid
executor_sys_rules: Call resume_line(customer_id, line_id) once. You may resume because all overdue bills are paid and the contract has not ended.
parse_rules: After 'Line resumed successfully' set task_state.line_suspended=false.

REBOOT_SERVICE.goal_template: reboot the device so the resumed line picks up service, then recheck

base_actions.when: the device has not been rebooted since the line resumed

executor_sys_rules: Step 1: call reboot_device (after resuming a line the user must reboot to get service).
Step 2: call check_network_status to confirm service is restored.
parse_rules: Set task_state.service_status ONLY from a check_network_status 'Cellular Connection: <v>' line.

DIAGNOSE_MMS.goal_template: check whether an MMS message can be sent
executor_sys_rules: Call can_send_mms with no arguments.
This is read-only; never apply a fix here.
parse_rules: The result is exactly one of two sentences: 'Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.' or 'Your messaging app can send MMS messages.'.
If the sentence contains 'cannot' (or 'not'), set task_state.can_send_mms=false and do NOT set resolved. This is the unresolved case — a fix subtask will run next.
ONLY if the sentence says 'can send' with NO 'cannot'/'not', set task_state.can_send_mms=true and resolved=true.

FIX_NETWORK_MODE_MMS.goal_template: set the network mode to include 3G+ (5G-preferred), then retest MMS

base_actions.when: the network mode was just set to 4g_5g_preferred

executor_sys_rules: MMS requires at least 3G. Call set_network_mode_preference with mode='4g_5g_preferred' if the current mode is 2G-only (or otherwise limiting).
After the mode is set, call can_send_mms to retest.
parse_rules: After 'Preferred Network Mode set to: 4g_5g_preferred' set task_state.network_mode='4g_5g_preferred'.

Read the can_send_mms result carefully: if it contains 'cannot' or 'not', set task_state.can_send_mms=false and do NOT set resolved. ONLY if it says the app 'can send' with NO 'cannot'/'not' anywhere, set can_send_mms=true and resolved=true.

CHECK_WIFI_CALLING.goal_template: check whether Wi-Fi Calling is ON (it can interfere with MMS)
executor_sys_rules: Call check_wifi_calling_status with no arguments.
Read-only; never apply a fix here.
parse_rules: From 'Wi-Fi Calling is currently turned ON/OFF' set task_state.wifi_calling_on (ON->true, OFF->false).

FIX_WIFI_CALLING.goal_template: turn Wi-Fi Calling OFF, then retest MMS

base_actions.when: Wi-Fi Calling was just turned OFF

executor_sys_rules: Call toggle_wifi_calling ONLY if task_state.wifi_calling_on is true (it flips state; call at most once).
After Wi-Fi Calling is OFF, call can_send_mms to retest.
parse_rules: On 'Wi-Fi Calling is now OFF.' set task_state.wifi_calling_on=false.

Read the can_send_mms result carefully: if it contains 'cannot' or 'not', set task_state.can_send_mms=false and do NOT set resolved. ONLY if it says the app 'can send' with NO 'cannot'/'not' anywhere, set can_send_mms=true and resolved=true.

CHECK_MMS_PERMS.goal_template: check the messaging app's storage and sms permissions
executor_sys_rules: Call check_app_permissions with app_name='messaging'.
Read-only; never apply a fix here.
parse_rules: The result lists granted permissions, e.g. 'App ... has permission for: storage, sms.' or 'has no permissions granted.'.
Set task_state.mms_storage_perm = true if 'storage' is listed, else false.
Set task_state.mms_sms_perm = true if 'sms' is listed, else false.

FIX_STORAGE_PERM.goal_template: grant the messaging app the storage permission, then retest MMS

base_actions.when: the storage permission was just granted

executor_sys_rules: Call grant_app_permission with app_name='messaging', permission='storage' (only this permission in this subtask).
After it is granted, call can_send_mms to retest.
parse_rules: After "Permission 'storage' granted" set task_state.mms_storage_perm=true.

Read the can_send_mms result carefully: if it contains 'cannot' or 'not', set task_state.can_send_mms=false and do NOT set resolved. ONLY if it says the app 'can send' with NO 'cannot'/'not' anywhere, set can_send_mms=true and resolved=true.

FIX_SMS_PERM.goal_template: grant the messaging app the sms permission, then retest MMS

base_actions.when: the sms permission was just granted

executor_sys_rules: Call grant_app_permission with app_name='messaging', permission='sms' (only this permission in this subtask).
After it is granted, call can_send_mms to retest.
parse_rules: After "Permission 'sms' granted" set task_state.mms_sms_perm=true.

Read the can_send_mms result carefully: if it contains 'cannot' or 'not', set task_state.can_send_mms=false and do NOT set resolved. ONLY if it says the app 'can send' with NO 'cannot'/'not' anywhere, set can_send_mms=true and resolved=true.

CHECK_APN_MMS.goal_template: check whether the APN's MMSC URL is configured
executor_sys_rules: Call check_apn_settings with no arguments.
Read-only; never apply a fix here.
parse_rules: From 'MMSC URL (for picture messages): <v>' set task_state.mmsc_url_present = false if it is 'Not Set' (empty), otherwise true.

RESET_APN_MMS.goal_template: reset APN settings to restore the default MMSC URL (applies on reboot)
executor_sys_rules: Call reset_apn_settings with no arguments. This restores the default APN (and its MMSC URL); it applies on the next reboot. Call it exactly once.
parse_rules: After 'APN settings will reset at reboot.' set task_state.mmsc_url_present=true.

REBOOT_MMS.goal_template: reboot the device so the APN reset takes effect
executor_sys_rules: Call reboot_device with no arguments so the APN reset is applied. Call it exactly once.
parse_rules: After the reboot output ('Restarting network services...') set task_state.apn_rebooted=true.

RETEST_MMS.goal_template: retest whether MMS can be sent after the APN reset + reboot
executor_sys_rules: Call can_send_mms with no arguments to check whether MMS works now.
parse_rules: Read the can_send_mms result: if it contains 'cannot' or 'not', set task_state.can_send_mms=false and do NOT set resolved. ONLY if it says the app 'can send' with NO 'cannot'/'not', set can_send_mms=true and resolved=true.

ESCALATE.goal_template: transfer to a human agent when the workflow is exhausted or the issue cannot be fixed by the agent

args.summary: <one-line summary of steps tried and the remaining issue>

base_actions.when: task_state.resolved == false and no remaining applicable fix

executor_sys_rules: Escalate only when no remaining applicable fix exists (e.g. a SIM locked with PIN/PUK, or a line whose contract has ended, or all relevant steps tried without success).
Provide a concise summary of what was checked and changed.
parse_rules: After 'Transfer successful' set task_state.escalated=true; this ends the workflow.

VERIFY_SERVICE.goal_template: final check: confirm cellular service is restored
executor_sys_rules: Call check_network_status once to confirm the final service state.
parse_rules: From 'Cellular Connection: <v>' set task_state.service_status; if it is 'connected' set task_state.resolved=true.

VERIFY_SPEED.goal_template: final check: confirm mobile data speed is excellent
executor_sys_rules: Call run_speed_test once to confirm the final data speed.
parse_rules: Read the run_speed_test result: 'Speed Test Result: <n> Mbps (<Desc>).' -> set speed_test to the <Desc> word (lowercased), data_available=true, and resolved=true ONLY if <Desc> is Excellent; 'Speed test failed: No Connection.' -> speed_test='no connection', data_available=false, resolved stays false.

VERIFY_MMS.goal_template: final check: confirm an MMS message can be sent
executor_sys_rules: Call can_send_mms once to confirm the final MMS state.
parse_rules: Read the can_send_mms result: if it contains 'cannot' or 'not', set can_send_mms=false (resolved stays false); ONLY if it says the app 'can send' with NO 'cannot'/'not', set can_send_mms=true and resolved=true.
